response:
to distort or warp your svg, you can apply a transform attribute directly to the entire svg element or to individual path elements inside it. for instance, adding transform="scale(1.2)" will enlarge the graphic, while transform="rotate(45)" will spin it around its center point. if you need more complex warping like skewing or shearing, you can use values like transform="skewX(20)" to slant the shape horizontally without changing its overall dimensions too drastically.

***

another powerful method involves using the preserveattribute to control how the coordinate system behaves when you apply these transformations. by setting preserveaspectratio="none" on the svg tag, you allow the graphic to stretch independently in width and height, which creates a distorted look if you change the width or height values. this approach lets you morph the circle and internal shapes into an oval or a stretched form while keeping the original path data intact for easy editing later.
